invariant: every earned reward is issued exactly once, or its failure is VISIBLE
pattern: Process Manager over a Transactional Outbox

synthetic_inputs.name: crash_fraction
synthetic_inputs.label: [SYNTHETIC] per-earn fault probability. Real incident rates are unobservable from outside; these exist only so the mechanism is bit-for-bit replayable — labelled exactly like the engine's '~18% omit cc_bin' synthetic. No Rokt-internal number is asserted.

synthetic_inputs.name: ambiguous_timeout_fraction
synthetic_inputs.label: [SYNTHETIC] per-earn fault probability. Real incident rates are unobservable from outside; these exist only so the mechanism is bit-for-bit replayable — labelled exactly like the engine's '~18% omit cc_bin' synthetic. No Rokt-internal number is asserted.

synthetic_inputs.name: hard_failure_fraction
synthetic_inputs.label: [SYNTHETIC] per-earn fault probability. Real incident rates are unobservable from outside; these exist only so the mechanism is bit-for-bit replayable — labelled exactly like the engine's '~18% omit cc_bin' synthetic. No Rokt-internal number is asserted.

grounding: The transactional outbox and its PENDING → PUBLISHED → DEAD_LETTER lifecycle are built and tested here (outbox.py). Applying it to reward issuance is [HYPOTHESIS] — the mechanism is real, the reward ledger is not claimed to be Rokt's. The idempotent consumer mirrors the real conversion dedup (conversions.py).
note: Same 200 earns, same seeded faults, two integration patterns. Dual-write leaves 33 earns silently diverged (20 orphaned, 13 double-issued); the transactional outbox leaves 0 — its 3 failures are dead-letters a human can see. The outbox mechanism is [REPO]; the reward application is [HYPOTHESIS]; the fault fractions are [SYNTHETIC] and labelled. Computed live, not asserted.
